name: Light
description: White or very light gray background with dark text and colored accents. Maximum readability for documentation and presentations.

name: Dark
description: Deep gray or near-black background with light-colored elements and bright accents. High contrast for digital-first or dark-mode contexts.

name: Neutral
description: Muted mid-tone palette with soft grays, beige, and slate. Understated and professional for formal reports and internal documentation.

name: Vibrant
description: Saturated, high-energy color palette with bold fills and bright contrasts. Ideal for presentations targeting non-technical or executive audiences.

name: Monochrome
description: Single hue or pure black-and-white palette. Clean and print-friendly, best for minimal, editorial, or high-contrast accessibility contexts.

name: Brand Blue
description: Corporate blue-dominant palette with white and gray supporting tones. Conveys trust, technology, and professionalism.

name: Warm
description: Warm orange, amber, and soft red tones with light neutral backgrounds. Energetic and approachable for workshop and team-facing materials.

name: Cool
description: Cool cyan, teal, and lavender tones with white or dark backgrounds. Calm, modern feel suited for technical and data-heavy diagrams.
